copdAssessment.name: COPD Assessment (CAT, mMRC, GOLD)
copdAssessment.description: Combine mMRC, CAT and exacerbations to find your GOLD 2023 group (A/B/E).

meta.title: COPD Assessment Calculator — CAT, mMRC & GOLD Group (A/B/E) | Health Calculators
meta.description: Score your COPD symptoms: mMRC dyspnea scale, CAT score (0–40), exacerbation history — automatic GOLD 2023 ABE classification. Free, anonymous, instant.

copdAssessment.title: COPD Assessment (CAT, mMRC, GOLD)
copdAssessment.description: Answer ten questions about breathlessness, daily symptoms and flare-ups in the past year. The calculator returns your mMRC grade, CAT score and GOLD group (A/B/E) per GOLD 2023.
copdAssessment.introNote: Reference period: current state and the past 12 months. Pick exactly one answer per question.

options.label: Grade 1 — When hurrying or walking up a slight hill
Grade 2 — Walk slower than peers, or stop on level ground
Grade 3 — Stop after 100 m or after a few minutes on level ground
Grade 4 — Too breathless to leave the house or while dressing

phlegm.low: I have no phlegm in my chest at all
phlegm.high: My chest is completely full of phlegm

chestTightness.low: My chest does not feel tight at all

breathlessness.low: When I walk up a hill or one flight of stairs I am not breathless
breathlessness.high: When I walk up a hill or one flight of stairs I am very breathless

activityLimitation.low: I am not limited doing any activities at home
activityLimitation.high: I am very limited doing activities at home

leavingHome.low: I am confident leaving my home despite my lung condition
leavingHome.high: I am not at all confident leaving my home because of my lung condition

sleep.high: I don't sleep soundly because of my lung condition

energy.high: I have no energy at all

moderate.title: How many moderate exacerbations (antibiotics or oral steroids without admission) did you have in the past 12 months?

hospitalized.title: Have you been hospitalized at least once because of an exacerbation in the past 12 months?

groupHints.A: Group A — few symptoms, low exacerbation risk. Initial therapy is usually a single bronchodilator. Continue regular review and trigger avoidance.
groupHints.B: Group B — many symptoms, low exacerbation risk. Initial therapy is typically dual bronchodilation (LABA + LAMA). Discuss adjustments with your clinician.
groupHints.E: Group E — high exacerbation risk (≥ 2 moderate or ≥ 1 hospitalization). Often combination therapy (LABA + LAMA, ± ICS if eosinophils elevated). Seek prompt clinical review.

categoryHints.veryHigh: Very high impact — broad daily limitation.

copdAssessment.incompleteHint: Answer every question to see your COPD assessment.

scaleRows.criteria: 0–1 moderate, no admission · mMRC ≥ 2 or CAT ≥ 10
scaleRows.label: Many symptoms, low risk

copdAssessment.howItWorksText: The calculator combines three validated tools: mMRC (dyspnea scale 0–4 per Bestall, 1999), CAT (8 items × 0–5, total 0–40 per Jones, 2009) and the exacerbation history of the past 12 months. Together they determine your GOLD 2023 ABE group — the basis of initial pharmacotherapy. The spirometric GOLD grade (1–4) requires lung function testing in clinic.
copdAssessment.disclaimer: This tool does not replace clinical diagnosis. A COPD diagnosis requires post-bronchodilator spirometry (FEV1/FVC < 0.70). For acute breathlessness, fever, or new purulent sputum, seek prompt medical care.

faq.q: What does the COPD Assessment calculator measure?
faq.a: It quantifies symptom burden (mMRC + CAT) and exacerbation risk (past 12 months) and assigns you to GOLD group A, B or E. These groups guide initial COPD therapy per GOLD 2023.

faq.q: What do the GOLD groups mean?
faq.a: GOLD 2023 uses three groups: A (few symptoms, low risk), B (many symptoms, low risk), E (high exacerbation risk). Group drives initial pharmacotherapy.

faq.q: What is the difference between mMRC and CAT?
faq.a: mMRC captures dyspnea only via five graded statements (0–4). CAT covers eight symptom and quality-of-life dimensions (cough, phlegm, energy, sleep, etc.) and is more sensitive. GOLD recommends CAT as the preferred tool.
An acute worsening of respiratory symptoms requiring a change in therapy. Moderate = outpatient antibiotics and/or oral steroids. Severe = hospitalization.
Yes — a COPD diagnosis requires post-bronchodilator FEV1/FVC < 0.70. This calculator stratifies risk in established COPD or signals when assessment is worth pursuing.

faq.q: How often should I repeat the assessment?
faq.a: Every 6–12 months when stable. Sooner with any therapy change, after an exacerbation, or with noticeable worsening. Trends matter more than a single value.

faq.q: What if I'm in Group E?
faq.a: Group E means high exacerbation risk. Discuss dual or triple bronchodilation with your pulmonologist, possible inhaled steroids (when eosinophils ≥ 300/µL), smoking cessation, and pneumococcal/influenza vaccination.
